objects.name: Analytic 1718
objects.description: Correlates (1) application interaction with elevation control mechanisms (e.g., Accessibility Service, Device Admin, overlay permissions, package installer flows), (2) rapid transition to elevated capability state without expected user interaction patterns, and (3) immediate privileged actions such as sensor access, UI manipulation, or background persistence. The defender observes a causal chain where an application gains elevated privileges through abuse of system-controlled consent flows and subsequently performs actions inconsistent with normal user-driven authorization.

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: android:MDMLog
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: application granted high-risk permission or special access (AccessibilityService, SYSTEM_ALERT_WINDOW, DeviceAdmin) with abnormal grant pattern (e.g., no recent user interaction or rapid sequence of grants)

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: MobileEDR:telemetry
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: application invokes privileged framework APIs (Accessibility events, UI automation, package install flows) immediately following permission grant

x_mitre_mutable_elements.description: Defines correlation window between permission grant and privileged behavior
List of permissions or access types considered high-risk (Accessibility, Device Admin, overlay)
Defines acceptable proximity of user interaction to permission grant
Baseline of legitimate apps expected to use high-risk permissions
